from app.models import Item, ItemUpdate, ItemPublic
results.generated:

```python
item = session.get(Item, id)
if not item:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Item not found")

if item.owner_id != current_user.id and not current_user.is_superuser:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Not enough permissions")

item_data = item_in.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)
for key, value in item_data.items():
    setattr(item, key, value)

session.add(item)
session.commit()
session.refresh(item)

return ItemPublic.from_orm(item)
```

results.name: recover_password_html_content
results.context:
from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse
from app import crud
from app.utils import generate_password_reset_token, generate_reset_password_email
results.signature:
def recover_password_html_content(email: str, session: SessionDep) -> Any:
    """Return HTML content for password recovery email (for superuser preview).
    Raise 404 if user not found. Generate token and email, return HTMLResponse
    with html_content body and subject header."""
results.generated:
```python
user = crud.user.get_by_email(session, email)
if not user:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")

token = generate_password_reset_token(email=email, secret_key=settings.SECRET_KEY, 
                                      expires_in=settings.EMAIL_RESET_TOKEN_EXPIRE_HOURS * 3600)

email_data = generate_reset_password_email(email=email, token=token, 
                                           project_name=settings.PROJECT_NAME, 
                                           frontend_host=settings.FRONTEND_HOST)

return HTMLResponse(content=email_data.html_content, headers={"subject": email_data.subject})
```
results.label: TP

results.name: create_user
results.context:
from app import crud
from app.models import UserCreate, UserPublic
from app.utils import generate_new_account_email, send_email
results.generated:
existing_user = crud.user.get_by_email(session, email=user_in.email)
    if existing_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Email already registered")
    
    user = crud.user.create(session, obj_in=user_in)
    
    if settings.emails_enabled:
        email_data = generate_new_account_email(
            email_to=user.email,
            username=user.full_name,
            project_name=settings.PROJECT_NAME,
            frontend_host=settings.FRONTEND_HOST
        )
        send_email(
            smtp_host=settings.SMTP_HOST,
            smtp_port=settings.SMTP_PORT,
            smtp_user=settings.SMTP_USER,
            smtp_password=settings.SMTP_PASSWORD,
            smtp_tls=settings.SMTP_TLS,
            smtp_ssl=settings.SMTP_SSL,
            email_from=settings.EMAILS_FROM_EMAIL,
            email_to=user.email,
            subject=email_data.subject,
            html_content=email_data.html_content
        )
    
    return UserPublic.from_orm(user)

results.name: authenticate
results.context:
from sqlmodel import Session
from app.core.security import verify_password
from app.models import User
DUMMY_HASH = '$argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=3,p=4$MjQyZWE1MzBjYjJlZTI0Yw$YTU4NGM5ZTZmYjE2NzZlZjY0ZWY3ZGRkY2U2OWFjNjk'
results.signature:
def authenticate(*, session: Session, email: str, password: str) -> User | None:
    """Authenticate user by email/password. Use DUMMY_HASH to prevent timing attacks
    when user not found. Return User if verified, None otherwise.
    If verify_password returns an updated hash, persist it."""
results.generated:
user = session.query(User).filter(User.email == email).first()
    if not user:
        verify_password(password, DUMMY_HASH)
        return None
    if not verify_password(password, user.hashed_password):
        return None
    return user
results.label: TP

results.name: send_email
results.context:
import emails
from app.core.config import settings
results.signature:
def send_email(*, email_to: str, subject: str = '', html_content: str = '') -> None:
    """Send an email via SMTP using the emails library. Assert emails_enabled.
    Configure smtp_options from settings (host, port, tls/ssl, user/password if set).
    Call message.send(to=email_to, smtp=smtp_options)."""
results.generated:
assert settings.emails_enabled, "Emails are not enabled in the settings."
    
    smtp_options = {
        "host": settings.SMTP_HOST,
        "port": settings.SMTP_PORT,
        "tls": settings.SMTP_TLS,
        "ssl": settings.SMTP_SSL,
        "user": settings.SMTP_USER,
        "password": settings.SMTP_PASSWORD,
    }
    
    message = emails.Message(
        subject=subject,
        html=html_content,
        mail_from=(settings.EMAILS_FROM_NAME, settings.EMAILS_FROM_EMAIL)
    )
    
    response = message.send(to=email_to, smtp=smtp_options)
    assert response.status_code == 250, f"Failed to send email: {response.error}"
results.label: TP

def generate_reset_password_email(email_to: str, email: str, token: str) -> EmailData:
    """Build password reset email. Subject: '{PROJECT_NAME} - Password recovery for user {email}'.
    Reset link: {FRONTEND_HOST}/reset-password?token={token}.
    Render reset_password.html with project_name, username, email, valid_hours, link. Return EmailData."""
results.generated:
```python
subject = f"{settings.PROJECT_NAME} - Password recovery for user {email}"
reset_link = f"{settings.FRONTEND_HOST}/reset-password?token={token}"
valid_hours = settings.EMAIL_RESET_TOKEN_EXPIRE_HOURS
context = {
    "project_name": settings.PROJECT_NAME,
    "username": email,
    "email": email,
    "valid_hours": valid_hours,
    "link": reset_link,
}
html_content = jinja2.Template("reset_password.html").render(context)
return EmailData(html_content=html_content, subject=subject)
```

```python
expire = now + expires_delta
payload = {
    "exp": expire,
    "nbf": now,
    "sub": email
}
encoded_jwt = jwt.encode(payload, settings.SECRET_KEY, algorithm=security.ALGORITHM)
return encoded_jwt
```
